법원은 조작기소 의혹을 불인정하며 민주당 주장을 배척했다.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Minister Who Dashed 5,000 Young Entrepreneurs' Dreams Now Heads to Prime Minister's Office
summaryTr: SME Ministry ministerial candidate faces responsibility for personal information breach affecting 5,000 young people
Large-scale data protection failure occurs in startup support business
Situation expands amid controversy over prime ministerial candidate qualification
editorials.bodyTr:
A minister who torpedoed the startup dreams of 5,000 young people is now heading to the prime minister's office. Han Sung-sook, who serves as minister of SMEs and Startups and is a candidate for prime minister, recently apologized, saying he deeply regrets and takes heavy responsibility for the massive personal information leak that occurred in the Ministry of SMEs and Startups' "Everyone's Startup" project. This incident represents a failure of national policy to protect personal information and the startup ecosystem that the government has been promoting as a key growth engine.

The "Everyone's Startup" project was designed to nurture 5,000 young entrepreneurs selected through competition, providing them with startup funds and mentoring. However, the personal information of all 5,000 participants was exposed to the internet, including their names, contact information, addresses, and even bank account numbers. This was not merely a data breach but a fundamental betrayal of the young people who risked their futures by participating in a government-led project.

What makes this situation even more problematic is Minister Han's response. A simple apology is insufficient. The young entrepreneurs whose dreams have been shattered deserve more than empty words. They need concrete measures to prevent identity theft and recover from potential financial losses. The government must establish a comprehensive support system and guarantee compensation for victims.

Furthermore, this incident raises serious questions about Minister Han's qualifications for the prime minister's position. How can someone who failed to protect a government project entrusted to his ministry now be trusted with leading the entire nation? Leadership requires not just apologizing for failures but demonstrating the ability to prevent them from happening in the first place.

The government must conduct a thorough investigation into the cause of the leak, hold those responsible accountable, and implement systemic improvements to prevent similar incidents. Until then, serious doubts will remain about whether Minister Han deserves to be promoted to prime minister.

editorial.body: 입력 2026.06.18 10:33 2026.06.18 09:43 2026.06.18 06:09 노태악 당시 중앙선거관리위원장이 지난 5일 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’ 관련 대국민 사과를 하고 있다. 뉴스1 중앙선거관리위원회가 투표용지 부족 사태를 초래한 6·3 지방선거 당시 일부 투표소에서 극심한 혼란상이 나타났던 것으로 확인되고 있다. 선거 당일 상황을 기록한 투표록에 따르면, 서울 잠실2동 한 투표소에선 투표관리관 도장이 찍히지 않은 채 용지가 배부됐다. 다른 투표소에선 바닥에 투표용지가 떨어져 있거나, 용지 부족 때문에 선관위에 전화를 걸어도 연락이 닿지 않았다고 한다. 추가 투표용지가 도착한 뒤에도 대기표를 받았던 주민들이 돌아오지 않아 참정권 행사가 불발됐다. 지난해 대선 때도 다른 사람의 선거인명부에 서명하고 투표한 사례가 2000건이 넘는 등 선거 관리 전반의 부실이 심각하다. 다 이유가 있었다. 선거로 업무가 바빠지는 해에 휴직자가 오히려 늘어나는 게 이번뿐 아니라 선관위의 전통이라고 한다. 이 조직이 공정한 선거 관리를 맡을 자격이 있는지 의문을 갖게 한다. 문제는 부실에서 그치지 않는다. 선관위 지도부부터 심각한 도덕적 해이에 빠져 있었다는 사실이 속속 드러나고 있다. 노태악 전 중앙선관위원장은 지난해 덴마크·스웨덴을 비롯해 재임 중 세 차례 간 해외 출장에 모두 배우자를 동반했다. 1200만원이 넘는 비즈니스 항공권 등의 경비를 선관위가 지불했다. 공식적인 외교나 국가 중대사도 아닌 해외 선거 관련 방문에 세금을 흥청망청 쓴 것이어서 외유성 출장이라는 비판을 면키 어렵다. 외부에 공개되는 출장 보고서에 ‘부부 동반’ 문구를 넣지 않은 것만 봐도 명백한 특혜가 아닐 수 없다. 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다. 선관위 직원들은 몰디브 등 세계적인 휴양지로 출장을 가고 선거 홍보물로 활용된다며 해변 사진을 버젓이 보고서에 첨부했다. 역량 강화를 이유로 이탈리아 등 관광지에도 매년 직원을 보냈다. 심지어 선관위 고위직들의 ‘자녀 특혜 채용’ 의혹이 불거진 시기에도 외유성 출장이 계속될 정도로 내부 기강 해이가 심각하다. 독립성을 방패 삼아 외부의 감시와 견제를 피해 성역을 구축해 온 선관위는 전면적인 개혁 없이는 국민의 신뢰를 회복할 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다. 예산 집행의 투명성 확보와 함께 채용 비리를 차단할 감시 시스템이 도입돼야 한다. 신뢰를 잃은 선관위가 관리하는 선거는 결과에 대한 불신의 씨앗을 남길 우려가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다.
summary: 6·3 지방선거에서 투표용지 부족으로 극심한 혼란 발생, 선관위 관리 부실 심각
노태악 전 위원장이 배우자 동반 해외출장에 1200만원대 세금 낭비
선관위는 독립성을 방패 삼아 감시를 피해온 후 전면 개혁 없이는 신뢰 회복 불가
editorial.stance: 선관위의 독립성 뒤에 숨은 적폐를 척결하고 예산·채용 감시 시스템을 도입해 민주주의의 기초인 선거 신뢰를 회복해야 한다
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: The Election Commission's Bottomless Moral Hazard in the Surveillance Gray Zone
summaryTr: Severe chaos erupted during the June 3 local elections due to ballot shortages, revealing serious negligence in election commission management
Former Chairman Noh Tae-ak wasted over 12 million won in taxpayer money on overseas business trips accompanied by his spouse
The election commission has evaded oversight by using independence as a shield, and trust cannot be restored without comprehensive reform
editorial.bodyTr: The National Election Commission has confirmed that severe chaos occurred at some polling places during the June 3 local elections that led to the ballot shortage crisis. According to voting records documenting the situation on election day, at one polling station in Jamsil 2-dong, Seoul, ballots were distributed without the election supervisor's stamp. At other polling stations, ballots were found scattered on the floor, and residents reported that calls to the election commission went unanswered due to ballot shortages. Even after additional ballots arrived, residents who had received waiting tickets did not return, resulting in lost voting opportunities. During last year's presidential election, there were over 2,000 cases of people signing other voters' names and casting ballots, indicating serious mismanagement across the entire election administration system. There were reasons for this. According to reports, it is a tradition at the election commission that the number of employees on leave actually increases during years when election work becomes busy. This raises questions about whether this organization is qualified to manage fair elections. The problem extends beyond mere negligence. It is becoming increasingly clear that the election commission's leadership has fallen into serious ethical lapses. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak accompanied his spouse on all three overseas business trips during his tenure, including visits to Denmark and Sweden. The election commission paid for business-class airfare exceeding 12 million won and other expenses. Using taxpayer money for overseas visits related to elections that were neither official diplomatic missions nor major state affairs is difficult to defend against criticism of junket travel. The fact that official trip reports did not mention "accompanied by spouse" makes clear that this was outright favoritism. Given the conduct of the agency head, employee misconduct was virtually inevitable. Election commission staff went on business trips to world-renowned vacation destinations like the Maldives and brazenly attached beach photos to reports, claiming they would be used in election promotional materials. They sent employees yearly to tourist destinations like Italy under the guise of capacity building. Internal discipline has deteriorated so severely that even when suspicions arose of "nepotistic hiring" among senior election commission officials, junket travel continued unabated. The election commission, using independence as a shield to avoid external oversight and checks while creating a sanctuary, has reached a point where it cannot recover public trust without comprehensive reform. Transparency in budget execution must be secured, and a monitoring system to prevent hiring irregularities must be introduced. Elections managed by an election commission that has lost public trust risk leaving seeds of doubt about their outcomes.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 공공기관의 도덕적 해이와 특권, 투명성 부족을 강하게 비판하며 감시 체계 강화를 요구하는 진보적 프레이밍으로, 권력 기관의 부실과 기강 해이에 대한 진보진영의 전형적 관점을 드러냄.

editorial.title: Readiness before politics
editorial.body: US Senate moves to tighten oversight of wartime OPCON transfer to Seoul The annual US defense bill recently submitted to the full Senate by the Senate Armed Services Committee includes a provision to strengthen congressional oversight of the transfer of wartime operational control to South Korea. The new provision in the fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act requires the US defense secretary, beginning in March next year and every 90 days thereafter through 2030, to submit reports to the relevant congressional committees on the progress of the wartime OPCON transition roadmap. It also requires the commanders of the US Pacific Command and US Forces Korea to each conduct an independent risk assessment of the wartime OPCON transition and submit it to Congress. This suggests that Congress intends to closely scrutinize the wartime OPCON transition process and exercise direct oversight of it. This also appears to reflect concerns in Washington that, amid the increasingly precarious security situation on the Korean Peninsula, wartime OPCON could be transferred for political reasons rather than based on military deterrence considerations. The United States is not opposed to the transfer of wartime OPCON. The issue is the differing views between Seoul and Washington on the pace of the transition. Last month, President Lee Jae Myung told Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back that "it would be accurate to say there would be no problem whatsoever even if wartime OPCON were transferred tomorrow," signaling his desire to complete the transfer before the end of his term. The Lee administration intends to seek to bring forward the transfer of wartime OPCON to next year at the annual South Korea-US Security Consultative Meeting later this year. However, the US view is different. US Forces Korea Commander Gen. Xavier Brunson said in April that South Korea and the United States aim to meet the conditions required for the transfer of wartime OPCON no later than the first quarter of 2029. Brunson said that "political expediency does not outpace the conditions" required for the transfer. Robert O’Brien, the former White House national security adviser who served as the top national security official during Donald Trump’s first term, said at a forum in Washington on June 18 that whenever politicians intervene and pull this issue (the wartime OPCON transfer) outside the military’s area of expertise, things tend to go awry. David Weiszol, deputy assistant secretary of state for East Asian and Pacific Affairs, said that the United States has consistently emphasized a conditions-based approach to South Korea to ensure that no vulnerabilities are left for adversaries to exploit. The Lee administration should heed these warnings from Washington. Currently, South Korea and the United States are conducting the second of three verification stages for the transfer of wartime OPCON. There is speculation that the final stage of the process — the Full Mission Capability assessment — could be brought forward despite the fact that the ongoing verification of the future Combined Forces Command’s Full Operational Capability has not been completed. However, even if Seoul and Washington reach an agreement, a wartime OPCON transfer that bypasses the verification process would be neither feasible nor desirable unless it can clear US congressional oversight. While wartime OPCON is a right that a sovereign nation should hold, its transfer must take place at precisely the right time, without creating even the slightest vulnerability in South Korea's security. North Korea's nuclear and missile capabilities continue to advance, and cooperation among North Korea, China and Russia is growing stronger. At times like these, the stability of the South Korea-US alliance and the combined defense posture must remain steadfast. While the transfer of wartime OPCON has symbolic value as a restoration of military sovereignty, what matters more is credible deterrence. A wartime OPCON transition driven by political considerations would be a highly dangerous gamble that could create a gap in the nation's security. The issue must be approached prudently, based solely on military capabilities and free from any political considerations. editorial.pullQuote: Political expediency does not outpace the conditions required for the transfer.
summary: US Senate tightens congressional oversight of wartime OPCON transfer to South Korea through 2030.
Seoul wants faster transfer by 2025; Washington insists on conditions-based approach by Q1 2029.
Congress warns against political expediency, demanding military readiness over symbolic sovereignty gains.
editorial.stance: Wartime OPCON transfer must prioritize military readiness and verified capabilities over political timelines.
summaryTr: 미국 상원, 2030년까지 한국으로의 전시작전통제권 이양에 대한 의회 감시 강화.
서울은 2025년 이양을 원하지만 워싱턴은 2029년 1분기까지 조건 기반 접근 주장.
의회, 정치적 편의 경고하며 상징적 주권 획득보다 군사 준비태세 요구.
editorial.bodyTr:
미국 상원, 한국으로의 전시작전통제권 이양 감시 강화 추진

미국 상원군사위원회가 최근 상원 본회의에 제출한 연간 국방수권법안에는 한국으로의 전시작전통제권 이양에 대한 의회 감시를 강화하는 조항이 포함되어 있다. 2027회계연도 국방수권법안의 새로운 조항은 미국 국방부장관이 내년 3월부터 2030년까지 90일마다 전시작전통제권 이양 로드맵의 진전 상황에 대한 보고서를 해당 의회위원회에 제출하도록 요구한다. 또한 미국 태평양사령부와 주한미군사령부 사령관이 각각 전시작전통제권 이양에 대한 독립적인 위험 평가를 실시하여 의회에 제출하도록 의무화한다. 이는 의회가 전시작전통제권 이양 과정을 면밀히 감시하고 직접적인 감시권을 행사하려는 의도를 보여준다. 또한 한반도의 안보 상황이 갈수록 불안정해지는 가운데 군사적 억지력을 고려하기보다는 정치적 이유로 전시작전통제권이 이양될 수 있다는 우려가 워싱턴에 존재함을 반영하는 것으로 보인다.

미국은 전시작전통제권 이양 자체에 반대하지 않는다. 문제는 이양 속도에 대한 서울과 워싱턴의 견해 차이다. 지난달 이준석 대통령은 안규백 국방부장관에게 "내일이라도 전시작전통제권이 이양된다고 해도 전혀 문제가 없을 것이라고 말하는 것이 정확하다"고 말하며 임기 말까지 이양을 완료하고 싶은 의사를 나타냈다. 이 정부는 올해 한미 안보협의회에서 전시작전통제권 이양을 내년으로 앞당길 것을 추진할 계획이다. 하지만 미국의 입장은 다르다. 주한미군사령관 자비에르 브런슨 대장은 4월 한국과 미국이 늦어도 2029년 1분기까지 전시작전통제권 이양에 필요한 조건을 충족하는 것을 목표로 한다고 밝혔다. 브런슨은 "정치적 편의가 이양에 필요한 조건을 앞지르지 않는다"고 말했다. 트럼프 전 대통령의 첫 임기 동안 백악관 국가안보보좌관을 역임한 로버트 오브라이언 전 백악관 국가안보보좌관은 6월 18일 워싱턴의 포럼에서 정치인들이 개입하여 이 문제를 군부의 전문 영역 밖으로 끌어낼 때마다 문제가 생긴다고 말했다. 국무부 동아태 담당 부차관보 데이비드 바이즈홀은 미국이 적들이 악용할 수 있는 허점이 남지 않도록 하기 위해 한국에 대해 조건에 기초한 접근 방식을 지속적으로 강조해왔다고 말했다. 이 정부는 워싱턴의 이런 경고에 귀를 기울여야 한다.

현재 한국과 미국은 전시작전통제권 이양을 위한 3단계 검증 중 두 번째 단계를 진행 중이다. 향후 연합사령부의 완전작전능력 검증이 완료되지 않았음에도 불구하고 최종 단계인 완전 임무 능력 평가가 앞당겨질 수 있다는 추측이 나오고 있다. 하지만 서울과 워싱턴이 합의에 이르더라도 검증 과정을 건너뛴 전시작전통제권 이양은 미국 의회의 감시를 통과하지 못하면 실현 가능하지도, 바람직하지도 않을 것이다.

전시작전통제권은 주권국가가 보유해야 할 권리이지만, 그 이양은 한반도 안보에 아무런 허점도 만들지 않으면서 정확한 시점에 이루어져야 한다. 북한의 핵·미사일 능력은 계속 고도화되고 있고, 북한·중국·러시아 간 협력은 점점 강화되고 있다. 이런 시점에 한미동맹의 안정성과 연합 방위태세는 흔들리지 않아야 한다. 전시작전통제권 이양이 군사 주권의 회복이라는 상징적 의미를 가지지만, 더 중요한 것은 신뢰할 수 있는 억지력이다. 정치적 고려에 따른 전시작전통제권 이양은 국가 안보에 공백을 초래할 수 있는 매우 위험한 도박이다. 이 문제는 순전히 군사 능력에 기초하고 정치적 고려로부터 자유로운 신중한 접근이 필요하다.

editorials.title: Readiness before politics
editorials.body: US Senate moves to tighten oversight of wartime OPCON transfer to Seoul The annual US defense bill recently submitted to the full Senate by the Senate Armed Services Committee includes a provision to strengthen congressional oversight of the transfer of wartime operational control to South Korea. The new provision in the fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act requires the US defense secretary, beginning in March next year and every 90 days thereafter through 2030, to submit reports to the relevant congressional committees on the progress of the wartime OPCON transition roadmap. It also requires the commanders of the US Pacific Command and US Forces Korea to each conduct an independent risk assessment of the wartime OPCON transition and submit it to Congress. This suggests that Congress intends to closely scrutinize the wartime OPCON transition process and exercise direct oversight of it. This also appears to reflect concerns in Washington that, amid the increasingly precarious security situation on the Korean Peninsula, wartime OPCON could be transferred for political reasons rather than based on military deterrence considerations. The United States is not opposed to the transfer of wartime OPCON. The issue is the differing views between Seoul and Washington on the pace of the transition. Last month, President Lee Jae Myung told Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back that "it would be accurate to say there would be no problem whatsoever even if wartime OPCON were transferred tomorrow," signaling his desire to complete the transfer before the end of his term. The Lee administration intends to seek to bring forward the transfer of wartime OPCON to next year at the annual South Korea-US Security Consultative Meeting later this year. However, the US view is different. US Forces Korea Commander Gen. Xavier Brunson said in April that South Korea and the United States aim to meet the conditions required for the transfer of wartime OPCON no later than the first quarter of 2029. Brunson said that "political expediency does not outpace the conditions" required for the transfer. Robert O’Brien, the former White House national security adviser who served as the top national security official during Donald Trump’s first term, said at a forum in Washington on June 18 that whenever politicians intervene and pull this issue (the wartime OPCON transfer) outside the military’s area of expertise, things tend to go awry. David Weiszol, deputy assistant secretary of state for East Asian and Pacific Affairs, said that the United States has consistently emphasized a conditions-based approach to South Korea to ensure that no vulnerabilities are left for adversaries to exploit. The Lee administration should heed these warnings from Washington. Currently, South Korea and the United States are conducting the second of three verification stages for the transfer of wartime OPCON. There is speculation that the final stage of the process — the Full Mission Capability assessment — could be brought forward despite the fact that the ongoing verification of the future Combined Forces Command’s Full Operational Capability has not been completed. However, even if Seoul and Washington reach an agreement, a wartime OPCON transfer that bypasses the verification process would be neither feasible nor desirable unless it can clear US congressional oversight. While wartime OPCON is a right that a sovereign nation should hold, its transfer must take place at precisely the right time, without creating even the slightest vulnerability in South Korea's security. North Korea's nuclear and missile capabilities continue to advance, and cooperation among North Korea, China and Russia is growing stronger. At times like these, the stability of the South Korea-US alliance and the combined defense posture must remain steadfast. While the transfer of wartime OPCON has symbolic value as a restoration of military sovereignty, what matters more is credible deterrence. A wartime OPCON transition driven by political considerations would be a highly dangerous gamble that could create a gap in the nation's security. The issue must be approached prudently, based solely on military capabilities and free from any political considerations. summary: US Senate tightens congressional oversight of wartime OPCON transfer to South Korea through 2030.
Seoul wants faster transfer by 2025; Washington insists on conditions-based approach by Q1 2029.
Congress warns against political expediency, demanding military readiness over symbolic sovereignty gains.
editorials.stance: Wartime OPCON transfer must prioritize military readiness and verified capabilities over political timelines.
summaryTr: 미국 상원, 2030년까지 한국으로의 전시작전통제권 이양에 대한 의회 감시 강화.
서울은 2025년 이양을 원하지만 워싱턴은 2029년 1분기까지 조건 기반 접근 주장.
의회, 정치적 편의 경고하며 상징적 주권 획득보다 군사 준비태세 요구.

summary: 이재명 대통령 국정지지율이 처음으로 부정이 긍정을 앞질렀다.
청와대는 민생경제 체감 악화가 종합적으로 반영된 결과라고 해석했다.
당·청 모두 국민의 우려와 요구를 더 세심하게 살펴야 한다는 경고다.
editorial.stance: 급락하는 지지율은 정부의 경제 정책 실패와 국민과의 소통 부족을 직시하고 근본적 개선을 촉구하는 신호다.
editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Plummeting Approval Ratings: A Wake-Up Call for Both Party and Blue House
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung's job approval rating has fallen into negative territory for the first time, with disapproval outpacing approval.
The presidential office interpreted the result as a comprehensive reflection of worsening public perception of the economy and livelihoods.
The outcome serves as a warning that both the ruling party and the Blue House must pay closer attention to citizens' concerns and demands.
editorial.bodyTr: A survey has shown negative evaluations of President Lee Jae-myung's state affairs management outpacing positive ones for the first time. The presidential office stated that this is "a result comprehensively reflecting the public's perception of the state of the people's livelihood economy and overall assessments of state management," and pledged to "pay even closer attention to what the people are concerned about and what they hope for.

summary: 이재명 대통령 국정지지율이 처음으로 부정이 긍정을 앞질렀다.

당·청 모두 국민의 우려와 요구를 더 세심하게 살펴야 한다는 경고다.
editorials.stance: 급락하는 지지율은 정부의 경제 정책 실패와 국민과의 소통 부족을 직시하고 근본적 개선을 촉구하는 신호다.
editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Plummeting Approval Ratings: A Wake-Up Call for Both Party and Blue House
summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung's job approval rating has fallen into negative territory for the first time, with disapproval outpacing approval.
The presidential office interpreted the result as a comprehensive reflection of worsening public perception of the economy and livelihoods.
The outcome serves as a warning that both the ruling party and the Blue House must pay closer attention to citizens' concerns and demands.
editorials.bodyTr: A survey has shown negative evaluations of President Lee Jae-myung's state affairs management outpacing positive ones for the first time. The presidential office stated that this is "a result comprehensively reflecting the public's perception of the state of the people's livelihood economy and overall assessments of state management," and pledged to "pay even closer attention to what the people are concerned about and what they hope for.

summaryTr: President Lee Jae-myung's approval rating has fallen for five consecutive weeks, triggering a death cross.

editorials.bodyTr: President Lee Jae-myung's job approval rating has fallen for five consecutive weeks, with negative evaluations now exceeding positive ones in what is being called a "dead cross." According to polling agency Realmeter on the 22nd, a survey of 2,517 eligible voters nationwide conducted from the 15th to 19th showed positive evaluations at 46.7 percent, down 4.8 percentage points from the previous week, while negative evaluations rose 5.5 percentage points to 49.7 percent. editorials.titleTr: From Troubled Firm to Korea's Market Leader...The SK Hynix Miracle
summaryTr: SK Hynix has risen to the top of South Korea's market capitalization rankings by ordinary shares.
It's a miracle of growth from a troubled company to the center of the global semiconductor industry.
One company's success should serve as a catalyst for other innovative enterprises to rise to the challenge.
editorials.bodyTr: SK Hynix has risen to the top of South Korea's market capitalization rankings based on ordinary shares. A company that once faced existential worries while undergoing a workout has now emerged as a pillar at the center of the global semiconductor industry. This is why SK Hynix's narrative of reaching number one in market cap should transcend the success of a single company and serve as a stimulus that awakens challenge and potential in other innovative enterprises. On the 22nd, SK Hynix surpassed 2,080 trillion won in market capitalization on the Kospi, outpacing Samsung Electronics.
